nodes.label: Service-Role Bypasses RLS; App Enforces Access
nodes.rationale: Backend uses the Supabase service-role key which bypasses RLS entirely. All access control is enforced in application middleware (requireRole.js, deviceAccess.js) instead of DB policies. Trade-off: simpler queries and one trusted server, but the service key must never reach the browser and there is no DB-level safety net if middleware is bypassed.
nodes.norm_label: service-role bypasses rls; app enforces access

nodes.label: Background Services Degrade Gracefully
nodes.rationale: MQTT, Web Push, and the timer poller all start even when their config (broker URL, VAPID keys) is missing, logging a warning instead of throwing. publishToDevice and logEvent are fire-and-forget. This keeps the HTTP API available regardless of broker/push availability, at the cost of silently dropped commands when infra is down.

nodes.label: profiles table
nodes.rationale: id references auth.users with on delete cascade; row auto-created by handle_new_user trigger.

nodes.label: household_members table
nodes.rationale: role check in ('admin','member'); unique(household_id,user_id) enforces many-to-many membership with one role per household.

nodes.label: devices table
nodes.rationale: household_id nullable so unpaired devices exist before assignment; stove_status, presence_status, absence_timeout_seconds(300), warning_delay_seconds(30) defaults.

nodes.label: join_requests table
nodes.rationale: status pending/approved/denied; reviewed_by and reviewed_at track admin review.

nodes.label: timers table
nodes.rationale: status active/cancelled/completed; ends_at not null.

nodes.label: events table
nodes.rationale: event_type text plus jsonb metadata; audit/event log written by backend service role.

nodes.label: push_subscriptions table
nodes.rationale: endpoint/p256dh/auth web-push keys; update via delete and re-insert.

nodes.label: handle_new_user trigger
nodes.rationale: SECURITY DEFINER runs as postgres bypassing RLS; set search_path='' prevents search-path injection; auto-creates profile on auth.users insert.

nodes.label: set_updated_at trigger
nodes.rationale: keeps devices.updated_at current on every update.

nodes.label: Row Level Security policy model
nodes.rationale: RLS enabled on every table; access gated by household_members membership and role='admin' checks via auth.uid().

nodes.label: profiles RLS (own-row select/update)
nodes.rationale: INSERT intentionally blocked; handle_new_user (SECURITY DEFINER) creates the row bypassing RLS.

nodes.label: households RLS (member view, admin update/delete)
nodes.rationale: any auth'd user can create; only members view; only admins update/delete.

nodes.label: household_members RLS (roster, admin manage, self-leave)
nodes.rationale: admins add/update roles and remove anyone; members can remove themselves (leave).

nodes.label: devices RLS (unassigned readable, member update, admin delete)
nodes.rationale: household_id IS NULL rows readable so pairing page can check status before assignment; any auth'd user can insert (pairing); admins delete.
nodes.norm_label: devices rls (unassigned readable, member update, admin delete)

nodes.label: join_requests RLS (requester+admin view, admin review)
nodes.rationale: no DELETE policy — requests kept for audit; re-request after denial done by backend via service key.

nodes.label: events RLS (member read-only)
nodes.rationale: written exclusively by backend via service role key; frontend clients can only read.

nodes.label: Design system (Clinical Precision)
nodes.rationale: cool steel-blue on near-white, hairline structure, light theme only; medical-device restraint.

nodes.label: Alarm color is earned
nodes.rationale: resting world is blue/green; amber and red reserved for genuine warning and danger so they retain meaning.

nodes.label: State-to-color safety map
nodes.rationale: online/presence/safe=green, stove-on-attended=blue, off/offline-secondary=gray, absence/buzzer=amber, auto-shutoff/OFFLINE=red.

nodes.label: State = color + icon + text label
nodes.rationale: never color alone — critical for colorblind users and the half-second glance (WCAG 2.1 AA).

nodes.label: Typography (Inter + JetBrains Mono)
nodes.rationale: mono with tabular-nums reserved for machine readouts (countdown, thresholds, pairing codes) so countdowns don't jitter.

nodes.label: Device card primitive
nodes.rationale: dashboard primitive: name+household, prominent status block (the half-second answer), secondary stats, actions; hairline border no shadow.

nodes.label: Warning-pulse signature motion
nodes.rationale: 1.6s amber pulse only when something is genuinely wrong; escalates to steady red on auto-shutoff; reduced-motion = static amber border.

nodes.label: Product brief (Hestia)
nodes.rationale: smart stove-safety system: Raspberry Pi watches stove, PWA gives remote visibility/control; brand vigilant, clear, reassuring.

nodes.label: Design principles (status is the product)
nodes.rationale: status is the product; alarm color earned; readable under stress in half a second; calm by default decisive in the moment; earned familiarity.

nodes.label: Accessibility & inclusion (WCAG 2.1 AA)
nodes.rationale: never encode safety state by color alone; reduced motion respected; 44px touch targets; generous type for older users.

nodes.label: Hestia PRD
nodes.rationale: Raspberry Pi + ESP32 CAM device with React PWA; local safety loop, MQTT, Supabase backend, Express on Vultr.

nodes.label: Local safety logic (absence/warning/shutoff)
nodes.rationale: all critical logic runs locally on the Pi over MQTT so stove shutoff does not depend on the cloud; absence timeout -> buzzer -> warning delay -> auto-shutoff.

nodes.label: NFC device pairing flow
nodes.rationale: NFC tag URL hestia.app/pair?device_id=...; new device -> add to/create household; already paired -> join request requiring admin approval.

nodes.label: Roles & permissions (admin/member)
nodes.rationale: admin has full management; member can operate stove features but not destructive/admin actions; enforced on backend not only frontend.

nodes.label: Household model (many-to-many)
nodes.rationale: user can belong to multiple households with a distinct role per household; household has multiple users and devices.

nodes.label: Event logging (event_type taxonomy)
nodes.rationale: named event types e.g. AUTO_SHUTOFF_TRIGGERED, JOIN_REQUEST_APPROVED, DEVICE_PAIRED; each carries household/device/user IDs, type, timestamp, metadata.

nodes.label: MQTT topic design
nodes.rationale: hestia/devices/{deviceId}/{status,presence,events,commands,settings,timers}; backend<->Pi<->ESP32 CAM.

nodes.label: REST API endpoints
nodes.rationale: Express verifies Supabase JWT; routes for households, members, join-requests, pairing, devices, stove control, safety-settings, timers, events, push.

nodes.label: System architecture
nodes.rationale: React+Vite PWA frontend; Express REST backend on Vultr; Supabase DB/Auth; Raspberry Pi + camera + ESP32 CAM local device.

nodes.label: Frontend README (React + Vite)
nodes.rationale: Vite React template; React Compiler not enabled.

nodes.label: index.html (Hestia PWA shell)
nodes.rationale: PWA meta (apple-mobile-web-app, theme-color #fbfcfd, color-scheme light), Inter + JetBrains Mono fonts, root mount, /src/main.jsx.

nodes.label: DeviceSummaryCard preview route
nodes.rationale: temporary route rendering DeviceSummaryCard across states: Attended, Stove off, Unattended, Warning, Auto shut-off, Offline; each shows Kitchen Stove, online/offline, Stove on/off, Presence detected/not-detected.

nodes.label: DeviceSummaryCard preview (2nd capture)
nodes.rationale: duplicate snapshot of the same DeviceSummaryCard preview route with the six state variants.

nodes.label: Phone preview (390x844 iframe)
nodes.rationale: iframe phone preview at 390x844 — resize window narrow for the real responsive app.

nodes.label: Caption: Phone preview - 390x844 - resize window narrow for real responsive app
nodes.norm_label: caption: phone preview - 390x844 - resize window narrow for real responsive app
